beats.title: Night in the Workshop
beats.boundaryReason: The session opens with table recap, character reminders, and the party deciding to hold the workshop through the night rather than push deeper into the manor after sunset. The whole beat stays focused on settling in, eating, and taking a long rest in relative safety.
dateEvidence: session.yaml sets drStart to 1748-10-17 and the beat begins on that evening
the DM opens by saying the sun is setting and the party is deciding what to do that evening
the beat ends with the party asleep after dinner, and the next transcript beat begins only when the party wakes before sunrise

beats.title: Dawn Plans and Chapel
beats.boundaryReason: The next beat begins with the party waking before sunrise, debating the safest route into the manor, and then committing to the chapel approach. From there the scene stays focused on the dawn infiltration across the vineyards and formal grounds until the party reaches the ruined chapel and uncovers the buried trapdoor into the crypt.
dateEvidence: the beat starts when the DM says the party wakes in pre-dawn light before sunrise
the route-planning conversation and the actual approach both happen in the same dim early-morning stretch
the DM repeatedly describes the light as dark, pre-dawn, or barely bright enough to move by
the beat ends at the moment the party drops into the chapel crypt before any later rest or date shift

beats.title: Ambush in the Crypt
beats.boundaryReason: After the party enters the crypt, the beat remains one continuous danger sequence: they scout the tomb, hear the hobgoblins above, set grease and snare around the hatch, kill the shadowsneak that drops in, reset the trap for a second pursuer, and only stop once the immediate fight is over and the crypt is quiet again.
dateEvidence: the fight begins directly after the dawn approach into the crypt and there is no pause or rest between the trapdoor discovery and combat
the transcript treats the two hobgoblin arrivals as one linked ambush sequence in the same crypt space
the beat ends with looting and checking the ring once the crypt falls quiet, still on the same morning

beats.title: The Hidden Manor Study
beats.boundaryReason: With the crypt secured, the party shifts into investigation: they loot relics and hidden supplies, find the secret passage out of the crypt, emerge in a sealed Perdoli study, decode the rotating bookcase, and search the hidden room thoroughly before setting up the next push deeper into the manor.
dateEvidence: this beat starts immediately after the crypt ambush ends and the party returns to searching
the party takes only a short rest in the hidden study, which keeps the action on the same date rather than advancing to another day
nothing in the study-search sequence suggests a later time-of-day change beyond the same morning infiltration

beats.title: Gallery Fight and Basilisk
beats.boundaryReason: The next phase covers the immediate tactical push out of the study: the party opens the secret bookcase, scouts the insect room and open gallery, draws hobgoblin attention, wins a quick indoor skirmish without alerting the manor, and then immediately discovers the chained basilisk in the courtyard when Gaudin risks a closer look.
dateEvidence: the beat begins as soon as the party uses the bookcase mechanism and starts choosing between nearby exits
the hobgoblin fight and the basilisk reveal happen back-to-back with no intervening rest or scene reset
the discussion after Gaudin begins turning to stone still reads as continuous same-morning aftermath

beats.title: Pirate Clues and Ghost
beats.boundaryReason: After surviving the basilisk's gaze, the party slows down to investigate the master chambers: they debate mirror tricks, read the Perdoli and pirate clues, recover the locket and letters to J.M., infer the history of the manor, and finally parley with the pirate ghost until he reveals Grimstone's hidden lair beneath the bedroom floor.
dateEvidence: this beat starts in the immediate aftermath of the basilisk scare, with the party still in the same run of manor exploration
the bedchamber search, paper review, and ghost conversation unfold as one uninterrupted final scene
the session ends on Grimstone's location reveal with no rest, meal, or date rollover after the morning infiltration
